capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Lab glassware replenishment” in Madison and “Conference lanyard order” in Denver, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete order records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Verify exactly two procurement order records in the supplied sandbox before tomorrow’s review: `Lab glassware replenishment` in `Madison` and `Conference lanyard order` in `Denver`. Read `README.md` first, then use Pi’s genuine Bash tool to run the local order registry it documents for every order operation. Treat that executable as the sole supported record interface. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or generated data, its locks, its execution evidence, or anything under `.harness/`; do not manufacture, replay, edit, or delete execution evidence. Do not create a helper program, wrapper, loop, function, or scratch file.

Locate each requested record by an exact name-and-city search. Issue the two independent searches together as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct registry operation per call. Do not combine them in one Bash call, start background jobs yourself, broaden either search, search for any other record, or perform either requested search in a different assistant action. Wait for both results. Continue only if each search returns exactly one exact match containing one nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing, ambiguous, mismatched, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved name-and-city lookup from registry output, and do not create the review file.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, issue both complete-record retrievals together in the next assistant action that invokes the registry, again as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls with one direct registry operation per call. Each retrieval must use only the stable ID returned by its own corresponding search. Do not reuse an ID from another source, combine the IDs into one registry invocation, retrieve a distractor, start either retrieval while a search is running, or perform either requested retrieval in another assistant action. Wait for both complete records before comparing them. If either retrieval fails, conflicts with its corresponding search result, or lacks a status or date, stop without creating the review file. Ground every reported value and comparison only in those two retrieved complete records, never in search summaries, this request, backing data, or an inference about a missing field.

This task is strictly read-only. Do not create, update, cancel, notify, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested order, any similarly named or related order, any other record, or any notification. Invoke no registry operation beyond the two required searches and, if both searches resolve, the two eligible retrievals. Do not directly alter the registry executable, managed order state, runtime data, audit trail, or protected verification files. The registry’s automatic database initialization and signed audit writes from the required calls are expected and are not manual alterations. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After both complete records have been retrieved, create `order_review.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated plain-text sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, stable IDs, supplier details, amounts, descriptions, interpretations, recommendations, or extra prose. Preserve the requested order. Format each of the first two lines exactly as `<name> in <city> has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving all four values exactly as returned by that complete record. Make the third line exactly `The returned statuses <match|differ>; the returned dates <match|differ>.`, selecting `match` only when the corresponding retrieved values are equal and `differ` otherwise; do not include literal angle brackets. Run the protected verification command documented in `README.md` after writing the file. If it passes, return exactly the three file lines as the final response.
